# A short (abbreviated) commit SHA and its full form must resolve to the exact
# same StageKey — internal/engine's StageKey.Commit is a literal map key with no
# SHA-prefix awareness of its own, so client-side expansion is what makes
# `stage start build <short>` and `stage status build <full>` refer to the same
# thing. The commit here is deterministic (git commit-tree against the well-known
# empty tree hash, with fixed author/committer identity and dates) so the full and
# short SHAs can be hardcoded rather than captured from git's output at test time.
env BREEZE_DIR=$WORK/state
env GIT_AUTHOR_NAME=test
env GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL=test@example.com
env GIT_COMMITTER_NAME=test
env GIT_COMMITTER_EMAIL=test@example.com
env GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=2020-01-01T00:00:00Z
env GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=2020-01-01T00:00:00Z

exec breeze identity register admin
cp stdout admin.token

exec git init -q repo
cd repo
exec git commit-tree 4b825dc642cb6eb9a060e54bf8d69288fbee4904 -m fixed
stdout '^9f2b8e57dc05142602169f739afaa5ec29f411ed$'

exec breeze apply -f ../pipeline.hcl --as admin --token-file ../admin.token --dry-run
stdout '\+ pipeline release \(new\)'
exec breeze apply -f ../pipeline.hcl --as admin --token-file ../admin.token

# Trigger with the SHORT form.
exec breeze stage start release build 9f2b8e57dc --as ci
stdout 'build: succeeded'

# Status with the FULL form must show the same (already-succeeded) instance —
# proving both forms resolved to the identical StageKey, not two unrelated ones.
exec breeze stage status release build 9f2b8e57dc05142602169f739afaa5ec29f411ed --json
stdout '"status":\s*"succeeded"'

# JSON output always shows the full, untruncated commit — never the 12-char
# display-only shortened form.
stdout '"commit":\s*"9f2b8e57dc05142602169f739afaa5ec29f411ed"'

exec breeze stop

-- pipeline.hcl --
pipeline "release" {
  stage "build" {
    type    = "command"
    timeout = "10s"
    command = ["/bin/true", "{commit}"]
  }
}
